observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:
a. Immediately implement the previously recommended one-time cleansing of
PPE accounts under COA Circular No. 2020-006, to avoid continued and
further misstatement of these accounts in the ensuing years;
b. Require the Inventory Committee to:
b.1 Regularly conduct a complete annual physical count of Property, Plant,
and Equipment (PPE); and
b.2 Ensure that the information contained in the Report on Physical Count of
Property, Plant, and Equipment (RPCPPE) is complete and based on the
actual physical count;
c. Direct the Municipal Accountant to:
c.1 Maintain property ledger cards to reflect the sufficient and particular
description of each PPE item;
c.2 Ensure that complete and accurate cost of road network assets is recorded,
including its lot and other components, to ensure that these are not
misstated in the financial statements;
c.3 Maintain the appropriate ledger cards for road networks following the
format in Annex D of COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 23,
2015; and
c.4 Make the necessary adjusting entries to recognize the correct amount of
Accumulated Depreciation of the belatedly recognized PPEs funded by the
LGSF of the DILG;
d. Direct the General Services Officer (GSO) to:
d.1 Maintain an updated Local Road Inventory and Road Map;
d.2 Prepare the Report on Local Road Network as provided in Annex A of the
above-mentioned Circular; and
d.3 Maintain a complete and updated property card for each item of the PPE
of the LGU based on the physical count;
e. Require the Municipal Accountant and GSO of the periodic reconciliation of
property records; and
f. Direct the Municipal Engineer to complete the inspection of the road network
assets and furnish the Municipal Accountant and GSO with the complete
description and cost segregation of all road projects.

We recommended that Management:
a. Direct the Municipal Treasurer to:
a.1 Include in the Report of Checks Issued (RCI) only those checks that have
been actually received by the payees as of the reporting date;
a.2 Ensure that all disbursement vouchers (DVs) are properly and completely
filled out with the required information to promote transparency and
facilitate efficient audit verification; and
b. Require the Municipal Accountant to record only in the RCI transactions that
are supported with complete documentation such as the official receipts (ORs)
to ensure the validity and reliability of reported disbursements.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to:
a. Prepare adjusting journal entries necessary to record the amount under the
appropriate accounts in compliance with the guidelines in Chapter 10 of the
PMNP Project Operations Manual; and
b. Recognize the receipt of the next tranches of PBG, if any, as an outright
income, and the subsequent utilization thereof under appropriate asset and
expense accounts in compliance with the provisions of the Operations Manual.

We recommended that Management:
a. Direct the MTO (Municipal Treasurer) to only include released checks in the monthly Report of Checks Issued (RCI) for submission to the Accounting Office; and
b. Require the Municipal Accountant to record the transactions that are supported with the same complete documentation such as the official receipts (ORs).

We recommended that Management strictly adhere to its responsibility in ensuring that the 20 percent EDF is well-appropriated at planned and procurement-and-implementation-ready, promptly implemented, and completed on target time to achieve the desired socio-economic and environmental outcomes for the beneficiaries.

We recommended that Management require the Local Risk Reduction and Management Office (LDRRMO) to:
a. Thoroughly analyze the needs of the Municipality based on the current situation and previous experiences in terms of disaster risk reduction and management to be able to identify the appropriate projects, programs, and activities (PPAs) to be included in the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund (LDRRMF) Investment Plan and the annual budget;
b. Judiciously and comprehensively plan the implementation of the identified PPAs to ensure that these are on schedule and the objectives are achieved during the budget year; and
c. Impose liquidated damages (LD), in accordance with Republic Act No. 9184, to contractors/suppliers who incurred delay in the delivery of the procured items.

We recommended that Management continue to implement programs aligned with the approved Solid Waste Management (SWM) Plan and further improve the LGU's compliance to the provisions of the Republic Act (RA) No. 9003.

We recommended that Management:
a. Strictly utilize the mandated Philippine Bidding Documents (PBDs) in all of its procurements for bidding; and
b. Submit to the Audit Team the complete documents required under COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009, within five working days from the execution of the contracts to enable timely and appropriate review of the same.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to:
a. Require for a standby letter of credit or guarantee instrument equivalent to the unrecouped advance payments to concerned contractors; and
b. Strictly observe the requirement of Annex E of the RIRR of RA No. 9184 on the required provision of irrevocable standby letter of credit of equivalent value from a commercial bank, a bank guarantee, or a surety bond callable upon demand, issued by a surety or insurance company duly licensed by the Insurance Commission, before making advance payments to adequately safeguard the interest of the government.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to:
a. Deduct the ten percent retention money from every billing up to 50 percent of work;
b. Impose additional retention on delayed and unsatisfactory works beyond 50 percent; and
c. Release the retention money only after final acceptance of the project or upon submission by the contractors of substitute guaranty equivalent to its amount.

We recommended that Management:
a. Require the Municipal Accountant to:
a.1 Review available copies of the Memorandum of Agreement, Trust Agreement, Trust Agreement, or Memorandum of Undertaking to determine the appropriate treatment of the receipts/collections of the LGU on their behalf; a.2 In the absence of the aforementioned documents, request clarification from the concerned national government agencies (NGAs) through the assistance of the implementing offices of the LGU, to determine the proper disposition of the collections; a.3 Prepare and submit the fund liquidation reports, duly approved by Local Chief Executive, of the completed programs/projects, and return the unexpended balances amounting to Php 532,108.94 to the source agencies to settle the accounts of the LGU in the books of the latter; and a.4 Coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer and exert diligent effort to trace the origin of the transactions to facilitate the return of unused funds to their respective source agencies and make the necessary adjustment, if any; and
b. Direct the implementing offices to timely implement the programs and projects funded by the transfers from the NGAs so that its objectives are immediately accomplished.

We recommended that Management:
a. Require the General Services Officer (GSO) to:
a.1 Immediately forward to the Municipal Accountant the Disbursement Vouchers for procured items together with the complete documents to support timely recording of its receipt and processing of payment or recognition of liabilities in the books;
a.2 Notify the Auditor of the time and date of the scheduled delivery within twenty-four (24) hours from acceptance to afford the latter to conduct its own inspection; and
b. Direct the Municipal Accountant to immediately record in the books all delivered items as supported by sufficient documentation from the GSO to ensure that the financial statements of the LGU are fairly presented.

We recommended that Management:
a. Direct the General Services Officer (GSO) to:
a.1 Prepare a complete Property Inventory Form (PIF) to provide the relevant and verifiable basis for the insurance of the properties of the LGU in subsequent year/s;
a.2 Submit the PIF to the Government Insurance Fund (GIF) of the GSIS not later than April 30 of each year; and
a.3 Furnish the Municipal Treasurer with a copy of PIF which shall be the basis for the updating, renewal, and application of new insurance coverage; and
b. Obtain full and updated insurance coverage for all the insurable properties listed in the PIF every year, without prejudice to the holding of the defaulting employees liable for the premium and fine, or withholding or suspension of the salaries in case of habitual neglect and unjustified non-compliance with the requirements under RA No. 656 and COA Circular No. 2018-002.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Health Officer and the Rural Health Physician to:
a. Submit the medical findings or certificate on the degree of injury sustained by the victims examined to determine the correct rate to be applied for the payment of honoraria; and
b. Re-compute the correct amount of honoraria for medico-legal services rendered, and refund the excess amount received amounting to at least Php 26,500.00 in the LGU accounts for CY 2024.

We recommended that Management:
a. Require the implementing offices, in coordination with the GFP, to judiciously plan the GAD programs and projects to effectively utilize the budget through the formulation of realistic activity proposals, among others;
b. Direct the GFP to closely monitor the implementation of the GAD programs and projects to achieve its objectives; and
c. Prepare the GAD Accomplishment Report based on the GAD Plan and Budget previously reviewed and endorsed by the DILG, fill out columns therein with appropriate and adequate information, and attach relevant supporting documents to facilitate the effective monitoring and evaluation of the GAD PPAs implemented during the year.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:


       a. implement the procedures prescribed under  COA  Circular  2020-006
          dated January 31, 2020 in the conduct of physical  count  of  PPE,
          recognition of PPE items found at station and disposition for non-
          existing/missing PPE items  for  the  one-time  cleansing  of  PPE
          account balances of government  agencies  to  produce  a  reliable
          report and PPE balances;


       b. direct the Municipal Accountant to record all  road  networks  and
          its components, based on the Registry of  Public  Infrastructures,
          or in its absence, based on  the  complete  Local  Road  Inventory
          Report provided with complete description and cost segregation  of
          the road components and provide  proper  disclosure  of  the  road
          networks in the Notes to Financial Statements;


       c. direct the GSO to maintain an updated  Local  Road  Inventory  and
          Road Map and keep a complete Local Road Network Property  Card  of
          all roads within its area of jurisdiction;


       d. direct the Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal  Accountant
          and the GSO with the complete description and cost segregation  of
          all road components within its area of jurisdiction;


       e. require the Inventory Committee to conduct complete  inventory  of
          local roads, highways and bridges within its area of  jurisdiction
          which were acquired thru purchase, construction or donations;


       f. require the Municipal Accountant to record  on-going  projects  in
          the appropriate CIP account to generate reliable balances  of  the
          CIP and related PPE accounts;


       g. strictly observe the use of Inventory Custodian Slip (ICS) by  the
          Property Officer in the  issuance  of  semi-expendable  items  for
          proper monitoring, control and accountability;


       h. require the Municipal Accountant to identify items  needed  to  be
          excluded from the PPE account  and  make  proper  adjustments,  if
          deemed necessary;


       i. require coordination  and  reconciliation  of  records  among  the
          Offices of the Municipal Accountant, General Services Officer  and
          Municipal Assessor for the accurate recording of  real  properties
          owned by the LGU; and


       j.  require  the  Municipal  Assessor’s  Office  to  facilitate   the
          registration of the untitled land and/or transfer of title of  the
          aforementioned parcels of lots in the name of  the  LGU  with  the
          Registry of Deeds.

We recommended that Management direct  the  Municipal  Accountant  and
Municipal Treasurer to monitor each bank account to avoid dormancy  of  bank
accounts.

a. require the conduct of physical inventory count in accordance with
          the procedures prescribed in the NGAS Manual for LGUs;


       b. ensure the preparation of RPCI and maintenance of SCs and SLCs  to
          facilitate  reliable  reconciliation  between  GSO   records   and
          Accounting Office records;


       c. implement Perpetual Inventory Method when recording  purchases  of
          inventory for stock as prescribed in the Manual;

       d. ascertain that all purchases and the issuances thereof,  including
          those paid thru reimbursements, are inspected by the Office of the
          GSO upon delivery/receipt, and are supported  with  AIP,  RIS  and
          SSMI.


       e. perform careful procurement planning to limit piecemeal  purchases
          and avoid resorting to reimbursements.

In view of the foregoing lapses, we recommended the Management to:


       a. stop the practice of recording financial assistance to IAs without
          the actual occurrence of inter-agency fund transfer;


       a. prepare an  adjusting  entry  to  write-off  financial  assistance
          totaling ₱3,900,000.00 recorded under Due from LGUs  and  Accounts
          Payable;

       b. send demand letters to barangays to require  full  liquidation  of
          financial assistance previously granted to them; and

       c. ensure that provision and liquidation of financial  assistance  to
          IAs are sufficiently supported with the  documentary  requirements
          prescribed in the existing rules and regulations.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to:


       a)  prepare  the  adjusting  entries  to  correct  the  corresponding
          liability and equity  accounts  amounting  to  ₱11,071,430.51  and
          refrain from premature recording of liability in  the  absence  of
          valid document/s showing proof of receipt of goods or services;


       b)  ensure  that  only   transactions   with   valid   and   complete
          documentations are recorded and make the necessary adjustments  in
          the books on the recorded payables not covered with  valid  claim,
          if there is any;


       c) send confirmation letters to creditors to prove the  existence  of
          these liability accounts; and


       d) ensure that claims are substantiated with complete  documentation,
          otherwise revert said balances to related account.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management direct:
a. the Municipal Accountant to retrieve the documents needed to trace the charges and make the necessary adjustments to reflect the true and accurate balances of the bank accounts for the KALAHI and DA funded projects;
b. the Municipal Accountant to revert to the General Fund or refund to the source agency the remaining balances of implemented projects funded by KALAHI and DA, when appropriate; and
c. the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to monitor each bank account to avoid dormancy of bank accounts.

We recommended that Management:
a. create a committee composed of one personnel each from General Services and Accounting Office who shall reconcile their respective records of PPE on a full-time basis and at the same time maintain updated and complete PPE Ledger Cards and Property Cards; and
b. in ensuing years, ensure that the conduct of physical count of PPE is in accordance with COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020, prescribing the guidelines and procedures in the conduct of physical count of PPE, recognition of PPE items found at station, and disposition for non-existing/missing PPE items, for the one-time cleansing of PPE account balances of government agencies.

We recommended that Management through the Office of the Municipal Accountant prepare the adjusting entries to correct its corresponding CIP, PPE, and Accounts Payable accounts amounting to ₱13.85 million, ₱7.15 million and ₱18.97 million, respectively, and refrain from the pre-mature recording of CIP or PPE in the absence of a measurable basis of recording.

We recommended that Management require:
a. the Municipal Accountant to revert back to the related PPE account the derecognized properties amounting to ₱12.88 million; and
b. the General Service Officer to make an inventory of all unserviceable properties in his possession and request for its disposition before derecognizing it in the books.
We further recommended that Management ensure that proper procedures in the disposal of government properties be followed before its value is derecognized in the books of the LGU.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant:
a. prepare the adjusting entries to correct the corresponding asset, liability, and equity accounts amounting to ₱6.12 million, ₱16.12 million and ₱9.99 million, respectively, and refrain from premature recording of liability in the absence of valid document/s showing proof of receipt of goods or services; and
b. ensure that only transactions with valid and complete documentations are recorded and make the necessary adjustments in the books on the recorded payables not covered with valid claim, if there is any.

We recommended that Management:
a. require the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit the fund liquidation reports pertaining to the completed programs/projects and return the unexpended balances amounting to ₱0.79 million to the source agencies in order that the account of the LGU in the books of the source agencies are settled;
b. discuss with the respective officials of the four beneficiary barangays of the funds received for site acquisition whether they are still interested to the project, if it turns out that the officials are no longer interested, return the amount of ₱300,000 to the source agency. With regards to the funds for piglet restocking program amounting to ₱14,840, return the fund if the program is no longer feasible; and
c. remit to the concerned NGA their shares on the local tax collected by the LGU amounting to₱0.59 million.

We recommended that Management require the submission of the following documents prior to approval of hazard pay:
a. approved list of position in its plantilla with the corresponding job description/actual duties that described the basis for justifying such positions who are exposed to occupational risks and assigned to places as stated in Section 16.1.1 of IRR of RA No. 9433; and
b. detailed daily accomplishment report, travel order and/or certificate of appearance of the MSWDO showing proof of actual exposure to hazardous situations to justify the payment of hazard allowance.

We recommended that Management in coordination with the Sangguniang Bayan issue policy guidelines on the disposition and payment of communication and internet allowances and consider the use of mobile phone fixed plan offered by telecommunication networks.

We further recommended that Management require:
a. the Municipal Accountant to ensure that all claims for communication allowances are supported with certification signed by the agency head or his duly authorized representative that the amounts claimed are used for official purposes only coupled with the Monthly Statement of Accounts/ used cell cards other than the proof of payments to substantiate consumption or usage; and
b. secure valid official receipts/invoices from the suppliers of the abovementioned transactions to properly substantiate payment of the same and submit the same to the Audit Team, otherwise, this shall be suspended in audit.

We recommended that Management:
a. submit the cited lacking supporting documents on the procurement of relief goods and gasoline to forestall issuance of Notice of Suspension;
b. require the Municipal Accountant to record expenses charged to the Trust Liability- DRRM funds following the accounting entries prescribed under COA Circular No. 2012-002; and
c. require the Municipal Accountant to record to the appropriate asset account the above enumerated assets amounting to ₱0.54 million and accordingly, compute and record the depreciation of the assets.

require the designated Inventory Committee and Accounting Office to prioritize the reconciliation of property and accounting records of PPE accounts and ensure the discrepancies noted in the reconciliation of PPE records be immediately verified and adjusted accordingly.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Treasurer to immediately return the unutilized funds to the NGAs concerned or request for appropriate authority to use said funds.

We recommended that Management:
adhere strictly with Section 3 of COA Circular No. 2009-001 at all times and submit/attach complete contract documents and required supporting documents to government disbursements; and
(b)	submit immediately the subject/needed lacking supporting documents to facilitate technical review and evaluation of the completed projects.
We recommended that Management require the full implementation of  programs, projects and activities covered by the LDRRMF annual plan and budget to achieve its objectives during the budget year.

We recommended that Management:
continue to formulate and adopt measures to intensify revenue collection efforts by strictly enforcing existing tax laws and ordinances within the Municipality; and
ensure the proper conduct of periodic assessment/evaluation of collection accomplishments in order to warrant the tapping of all sources to maximize the LGU’s revenue-generating powers mandated by law.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Acting Municipal Treasurer to submit a copy of the Report or Summary of the Uncollected Balances of the lessees as at CY 2018 to the Office of the Municipal accountant to reflect the true and correct amount of receivables, liability, and income of the LGU as of the period.

(a)	strictly conform to the generally accepted accounting principles in recording transactions to ensure accurate and proper presentation of account balances in the financial statements; and

(b)	prepare the adjusting entries to correct the Expenses accounts and the Accounts Payable account and observe the correct entry for the succeeding period.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the General Services Officer to:
(a)	submit written justification and the Statement of Accounts in lieu of the Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption and RIS for PY 2017 consumptions as abovementioned to forestall the issuance of Notice of Suspension;

ensure the completeness of the submission of the documentary requirements consistent with the COA Circular No. 77-61 for this particular transaction;

prepare adjusting entry to correct the Fuel, Oil and Lubricants Expenses account of the amount obligated as at the yearend; and

refrain from reporting purchase of fuel as an outright expense without the abovementioned required support documents. It shall be reported as an inventory and shall only be reported as expenses upon submission of the required support documents for the consumption.
